topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The string quartet swelled, a saccharine sound that grated on my nerves. I clutched my glass of champagne so tightly my knuckles were white, focusing intently on the meticulously arranged floral centerpiece across the room. Then, a familiar silhouette cut through the periphery of my vision. Liam. He was laughing, a bright, easy sound that seemed entirely too effortless for the man I used to know. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp constriction in my chest. I forced a smile, a brittle, thin thing that didn't reach my eyes, and steered myself toward the buffet, needing the distraction of food to anchor me. When he finally turned, our eyes met for a fleeting second. The warmth I’d been trying to suppress evaporated, leaving a cold, heavy emptiness settling in my stomach.

[story 2]

I was nursing a lukewarm cocktail, trying to appear engaged in a conversation about obscure pottery techniques, when I saw him near the dance floor. It was impossible to look away. The sight of him, so polished and undeniably happy in this setting, sent a jolt of unwelcome recognition through me. My hand started to tremble, and I quickly lowered my glass, the ice clinking loudly against the crystal. I watched him for a long moment, cataloging every gesture, every relaxed posture, and felt a profound settling of weight in my shoulders. When he finally glanced my way, a flicker of something—recognition, perhaps regret—crossed his face. I deliberately turned my attention to a passing waiter, focusing on the silver tray, my jaw tight, the muscles in my neck straining.

[story 3]

The noise of the celebration was overwhelming, a cacophony of forced merriment. I was standing near the balcony, trying to find a vantage point away from the main throng, when I spotted him. He was talking animatedly with someone, his posture open and unguarded. A sudden, visceral tightening gripped my throat. I felt a distinct need to disappear, to shrink into the shadows. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to steady the rapid rhythm of my pulse. When he finally looked toward my general direction, his gaze lingered, and for a beat too long, I felt exposed. I simply turned my back, the movement stiff and deliberate, and walked toward the exit, the sound of my own footsteps echoing the sudden hollowness inside me.
